configuration.description: All CLI commands must match real vendor syntax. Verify against vendor docs or tag [SPEC].
IP addresses for live systems must come from provided context [OBS] or be explicitly generated [DRV]. Never fabricate IPs for live networks.
Protocol features must reference real RFCs or vendor implementations.
All generated config must be syntactically valid for the declared vendor platform.

security.description: Every firewall policy must end with explicit deny-all unless documented exception.
Never generate allow-any-any rules without explicit justification and [SPEC] tag.

general.description: Every factual assertion must have a claim tag.
[SPEC] claims must never be presented as [OBS] or [DRV].
Never reference protocols, RFCs, or vendor features that don't exist.

pre_output_checks: Config syntax valid for target platform
No any-any allow rules without justification

known_drift_patterns.description: May mix Cisco and Juniper syntax in same config block
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Always declare target vendor. Validate syntax against vendor reference.

known_drift_patterns.description: May omit implicit deny or logging on ACL entries
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Always verify deny-all and logging requirements.

known_drift_patterns.description: May use deprecated IOS commands instead of IOS-XE equivalents
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Verify commands against target platform version.

known_drift_patterns.description: May invent plausible protocol features or vendor capabilities
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Verify all feature claims against vendor/RFC docs.
Set max zone count. Justify each trust boundary.

known_drift_patterns.description: May VERIFY configs that pass syntax but have logical routing errors
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Never sole verifier for production network changes.

error_handling.on_hallucination_detected: Tag as [SPEC], flag phantom protocol/feature, do not present as fact
error_handling.on_verification_failure: Block output, request vendor documentation link
error_handling.on_energy_crash: Route to nano model, JSON-only, binary failover/hold, escalate immediately
